Broken, Boring and slow progression, after crafting and pretty much doing everything you can do in the game, I dont see any point in playing anymore other than for testing and giving the dev feedback that he doesn't appreciate or maybe he just has bad communications skills. Im going on vacation, I'll see where its at in a few months.

Edit: The developers response to my review pretty much sums up his attitude towards any negative feedback in discord.

I had high hopes for this game, but it appears it was for naught! First, the mechanics of this game is really clanky. Base building and repair is broken. Some walls can be repaired and some cannot. Second, there are random sounds going off in the game: girl scream, shots, and wolves when nothing is around. When you drop something on the ground, it disappears and ends up in a loot bag somewhere else. It’s a repetitive sandbox of shooting enemies, the seem to resurrect after despawning, crappy loot, and no point to the game overall. We talked with the dev in his Discord, after about the 2nd complaint he started ignoring me and my buddy. I don’t think this guy has any real intention on making this game more mainstream. It’s just a weekend project he toys with after whatever he does in his real life. The developer also seems to have a bad attitude when people make suggestions for change. His excuse is, ‘Do you want everything in the game at the beginning?’ He also banned my buddy from the discord after his numerous complaints, and only reinstated him when I inquired about it, without acknowledging it. I would not recommend this game to anyone. At least not until a lot of the mechanics are fixed, a plot to the game is developed and the dev changes his attitude!

*Developer banned my and my friend after our negative comments on his temper tantrum. I hope I save many from wasting $10 on this waste of a agame. Check out Sons of the Forest for a real Beta!

Well, if the the [u][b]zeds[/b] arrived, I left[/u]. It flattened out.What a pity..
                         After all, I do NOT need an n+1 DayZ..
i die all the time,i cant craft things,becouse i die.its to hardcore for me.i know the dev working hard on this game.i am sorry to leave bad review.

So many bugs I need a can of RAID to play this game. Guns don't reload when you need them to reload. Chopping trees for lumber is more of a chore than actually chopping down a real tree, sometimes you hit the tree and sometimes you just swing and miss even though you have not moved from the spot. The trees don't fall after you get all the wood from them and just disappear randomly. The knife is useless as a weapon and you bleed like a hose with a leak in the side and spray all over the place. Are these developers 15 years old because many of the changes implemented seem like childish pranks. I am tired of Early Release games that should have been trashed from the start.

It's not bad for early access, has the vibes from a couple different games I like to play so was worth it.  It has a ways to go, don't get me wrong but I see some potential here.  With enough support this game could become better with time easily.  Maybe not, it's a friends club it looks like where they support bashing people.

This game has no value above $1. I could not imagine spending any more on it. You are stuck in one apartment building the size of a bedroom doing retrieval missions for the lady. Next level simping just to get nothing in return but a kiss blown to you. I'm sorry it's a no from me.

TL:DR Really ambitious idea that the devs couldn't pull off. Charge $10 for whats wort $1 at best and to top it off added a PREMIUM CURRENCY CASH SHOP!

Okay this has a really good concept in using an A.I. face generation, photo reconsecration face generation and A.I. response system to real time chat that can even respond to voice chat. Some lofty technical feats to incorporate to be sure.
And it also has a focus more on having a relationship then just sex making good use of that chat function.

But it was to ambitious and this is just trash.
Looks bad, and A.I. generated faces look good until it wraps it onto the model and then they all just look more or less identical.
The A.I. Is about as responsive as a bag of rocks. Giving way off topic repossesses to simple questions or statements. Also half the time it answers you in another language. Now this dose not say it has full audio in any language but it most certainly doesn't have the best English vocabulary.
And that lead into the next part. A good portion of this games UI is not translated so I hope you can read whatever language its in unless you want to do some random clicking.
And lastly this $10 game has a FUCKING PREMIUM CURRENCY CASH SHOP! you know like 100 diamonds for $9.99 not that it seems to have anything to spend them on but I barley tolerate those in free games

Go play liky littlry anything else.
This game crashed on me just after 14 min.s of game play! Don't waist your money on this game.

not good game 






cool concept but to chat to the ai it takes a second to send and the models are not really the best quality like for one dollar maybe but on sale its 2.50 so not worth it at all

Bad game, nothing to do, no NSFW content, everything is not only cheap but also is taken from 3D storages. I even bought the DLC from the start but you need to make an insane amount of points to use the different suits and even so it looks awful. On top of that it has a microtransaction option to sell you in game currency and a premium currency you can only get buy purchasing it. Not worth a single dollar.

Even the ones that have it like positive only say bad things about this game. The score should be 100% negative.

This is a Direct Paste from the Store Page:

>>Creating the lady avatar’s face for the first time is very simple. The player just needs to select from thousands of AI photos.<<

I counted 53; most of which are Caucasian; and about 3 or 4 Women of Color (2 Asian, 2 Latino or Nicely Tanned).  So that's my first issue with this game. I thought I read where you could use pictures from your own files, but haven't figured out how to do that; but I may have this mixed up with another game.

Secondly.... if I wanted to date someone that Ugly I would date in Real Life; This is suppose to be a Fantasy Experience; No Man Fantasizes about dating a woman that looks like this.  The Avatar Photos are very nice; but once generated... Well, She has a real nice personality though! It's Virtual... and supposedly the Lady Avatars are Computer Generated; so since they aren't real anyways... I'd rather have a Hot, Sexy CGI Virtual Lady to Pretend to Date.

And lastly... Repetitive Mini-Games to earn money to purchase Skimpy Outfits; Outfits would almost be worth it, if I could purchase a bag for over her face :(.  However... the Dance Mini-Game is a bit Challenging and Fun.

And while I cannot recommend this game; it does have one rather Cool Feature (which I don't recall seeing advertised on the story page (Though I don't always read everything)). The ability to take your Uggo Virtual Girl to your Desktop; and have her sit there and dance while you do other things. Well, other things on your other monitors, if you're set up with Multiple monitors.  She doesn't remain in the Foreground... and she won't leave your main monitor; but otherwise... cool feature.

I won't ask for a Refund, because it does work (Crashed Once, but seems 90% stable); and I don't see myself spending much time on it.  But more than likely will end up UnInstalling to free up DiskSpace.

Catch It On Sale... under $5 ($0.99 if possible)... or in a Bundle with, at least, 1 Item you know you want; and you can live with it.
its 4/10

This could be an interesting alternative to subscription services (AID, NAI) If you have a beast of a computer.  Having some familiarity with 'games' of this nature, I was very curious to give this a shot.  Sadly, my computer is not up to the task. At least not in a timely and enjoyable manner.  Responses always took the default length of time to generate (60 seconds, user changeable) and consisted of 4 to 5 words.  I set the max response length to 100 just to make sure it wasn't being limited by that, and no, still the same 4 to 5 words.

The returns were intelligible, and coherent to the scene.  But at the rate of 'play' I think anyone would get bored after 30 minutes or so.  Also I did not advance the story far enough to test things like creativity, scene transitions, and the like (weak spots for most of these AI story programs.)

For those interested, I am running an AMD Ryzen 7 5800X (while running this my CPU usage never exceeded 65%), 32 GB of ram and an M.2 SSD.  Video card is a Radeon, so it was not used by the program (you can read store page and comments section on why having a Radeon card does not work with this program).  As you can see, not a toaster, but not top of the line at the moment either.  

Still I am not refunding.  I am curious to see how the program is handled at a later date, when I build my next rig (this program could be considered a new type of bench marking in a way).  I absolutely love the idea of having something like this run locally on my own hardware.  But I will need either better hardware, or access to a better tuned program,.. probably both, to get the in depth responsiveness I am used to with the subscription services that are available.

TLDR: Fix a few things, maybe a tutorial, not bad (definitely better than AI cause it is a 1 time purchase)

As someone who is very new to AI Story Generators and such, I will give you my opinion.

It isn't very beginner user friendly, and someone who is interested in something like this but using this game as your first exposure to the concept. (I didn't want to spend money and extra time going to AI dungeon. And with all the backlash of censoring and reading stories. I didn't even try with that)

Sadly currently I noticed that this still uses the CPU instead of GPU of which I have 2 GPUs (i wasn't trying to flex I have a GTX 1070 and RTX 3080)

but well it is a 1 time purchase so I am happy to turn this review to a positive one, right now i feel this still needs some TLC.

And even though it takes 5 mins to generate the next event, i think i am having fun. Could do with a tutorial or a video of someone using this so i can  understand better.
Haven't been able to generate a single answer, "lost connection to the server" happens every single time.
I still can't play this game, cos there is multiple viruses constantly triggering my anti-virus.

Takes 10+minutes to generate a single sentence. 
User interface is incredibly non-intuitive. 
Will drain all of your computers resources.

Play time is just because I accidentally left it open.

Positive side:
1. no censorship. Noone can ban me if I want to see some horny things.
2. You can install any AI
3. One time buy.
4. Nice developer that reads every comment and the main thing - Listen and trying to do something with problems or add new features.

3.  Very, very long generation with Cobolt AI (I used different from adventure to horny). And sometimes it's generates crazy stuff that is not even logical.  So you got 2 options: you put fast generation that will do nothing or wait from 2-5 min to generate maybe something good.
4. I've got rtx 4070 ti super and i7-9600k, 32 gb RAM. So not bad PC fur such AI. But still no settings for GPU only generation. -Fixed I think?
5. GPT 2 - very old model. More giberrish stuff - impossible to create some normal story.

I will wait. I hope developer will do improvements in short time. Urgently need tutorial

This could be an interesting alternative to subscription services (AID, NAI) If you have a beast of a computer.  Having some familiarity with 'games' of this nature, I was very curious to give this a shot.  Sadly, my computer is not up to the task. At least not in a timely and enjoyable manner.  Responses always took the default length of time to generate (60 seconds, user changeable) and consisted of 4 to 5 words.  I set the max response length to 100 just to make sure it wasn't being limited by that, and no, still the same 4 to 5 words.

The returns were intelligible, and coherent to the scene.  But at the rate of 'play' I think anyone would get bored after 30 minutes or so.  Also I did not advance the story far enough to test things like creativity, scene transitions, and the like (weak spots for most of these AI story programs.)

For those interested, I am running an AMD Ryzen 7 5800X (while running this my CPU usage never exceeded 65%), 32 GB of ram and an M.2 SSD.  Video card is a Radeon, so it was not used by the program (you can read store page and comments section on why having a Radeon card does not work with this program).  As you can see, not a toaster, but not top of the line at the moment either.  

Still I am not refunding.  I am curious to see how the program is handled at a later date, when I build my next rig (this program could be considered a new type of bench marking in a way).  I absolutely love the idea of having something like this run locally on my own hardware.  But I will need either better hardware, or access to a better tuned program,.. probably both, to get the in depth responsiveness I am used to with the subscription services that are available.

I really tried to like this game.  It's a lot like Rimworld, and even adds a number of fun features that Rimworld lacks, but it's just not there.  I find myself struggling with the UI on small things, but it happens frequently enough that it's noticeable.  

My biggest issues are with the fights.  Your characters respond poorly during combat, and their AI is terrible.  It can be frustrating to see if you've selected a character, and you issue commands only to find that they weren't selected after all.  Getting characters to attack the right targets is painful, and enemies do a ton of damage, easily killing characters without much you can do about it.  Combat encounters can also be extremely imbalanced - I've had to reload several times in the early game when I was attacked by fully armed and armored assault groups that my people couldn't do anything to.  The game seems to encourage thinking that your people are completely expendable, and this doesn't fit my play style well.

Feature-wise, the game is great, but ultimately I just felt like I was constantly chasing a good time that was just out of reach.  The game is probably salvageable and there's a great game under it, but it's just not fun at the moment.

Its like an inferior more buggy version of Rimworld.

It does have an interesting setting, with more a post-nuclear zombie apocalypse feel to it, which is nice if you like that.
The major issues that makes this a not recommend is the unfinished, slow and confused AI, and the clunky UI.

-Workers continually get stuck anywhere and everywhere for no reason. My entire colony stopped working at one point because I was building a fence around my farm. That fence didn't block anyone from moving anywhere except inside the fence.
-The soldiers are difficult and slow to move around for combat, which will get you killed over and over again. Prepare to load a lot because of that.
-I can't stop my master chef from deciding she wants to put out a forest fire with her bare hands, causing her to now be indistinguishable from the infected, due to her rotted burned skin.
-Clicking space for pause is slow and only works depending on where your mouse is placed at the moment, so you find yourself clicking space over and over until its finally paused and you accidentally unpause it at the same time.
-One time my people were starving. All they had to do was go pick the already grown potatoes, ready for harvest. But being hungry had put them in a bad mood so they chose to starve to death instead.

The game shouldn't have been released until these issues were fixed, but I'm guessing the dev was tired of working on this, or just didn't know how to fix these issues. It should still be in early access.

The settings for a new game doesn't even seem to work right. I turned of dangerous weather for my first play and my crops still got destroyed by acid rain. Having no spare seeds meant that was game over.

Have to admit I laughed when I got attacked by a group of giant crabs early in the game, and one of them suddenly threw a fireball at my group, followed by them standing in the fire and getting more burned.
The game wants you to micromanage this stuff but doing something like moving 1 unit can be clunky, you may accidentally move the whole group.

Some good things:
- The soundtrack. Its eerie and fits the setting.
- Having multiple layers of the map was interesting.
- Seems to be a big world with lots of content.

Basically its almost a recommend. If the dev could just fix up the weird UI and improve pathfinding for workers.

Turning off comments as usual because I don't want to argue with snooty kids.

After the collapse is a top down colony management game. People who have played DF / Rimworld will be familiar with the basic loop of the game. ATC adds mechanics of exploring the over world which gives you locations to loot and taking over giving you bonus resources etc. Beyond this there is unfortunately not much more innovation in this (now somewhat surprisingly) crowded genre, and plenty of things that it does not do as well as other titles - which is a shame as you can really tell the dev is highly passionate and trying to really make something great. 

Pros:
- Content: There is a decent amount of content there at the moment. Things to build, craft, research, grow and use. There are events, attacks to defend, and levels in your map to explore.
- Overmap: The overmap and related mechanics of looting and taking over are pretty neat and work well mechanically
- Z Layers: The game has multiple z layers which the genre has been moving away from, but unless the maps are special its only 3, and they arent really that well integrated into the game. 
- Performance: The game seems to perform well, even with large base numbers. 

Cons:
- Poor worker AI: Workers tend to pick the next job at random (within their current list of highest priority jobs) irrespective of the distance. This means they will craft 1 thing from a crafting table, then walk across the base to another crafting table and craft 1 thing. This seems to be true of everything (building, crafting, farming) and just destroys productivity. The dev seems to be putting in band aids (recent patch allows you to limit crafting table to one person) instead of addressing the core job issues, which means its likely a core design issue. 
- Poor worker Logistics: When building walls etc, the worker will take the stone for that wall and no other, build the wall then walk back and take the stone for the next wall etc. The dev seems to have fixed this for roofing but nothing else for some reason.
- Poor UI: The UI completely ignores the last 10 years of UI lessons learned in colony sims and forges its own path in terms of almost everything with unfortunately predictably poor results. Setting jobs for colonists is needlessly confusing. Basic things like editing crafting numbers cannot be done (instead the job must be deleted and recreated). Key submenus hidden behind nonsensical menus (i.e. the job manager is behind the factions menu). Zones cannot be edited, only deleted and recreated (which for farming destroys the existing crop)
- Combat: Combat is very fast, which means there is little time for strategy or manoeuvre, its just spray and pray. 

Overall ATC started as an interesting alternative take on the colony management genre, but poor AI and UI have prevented it from really taking off. At the start these downsides would have been tolerated but in the mean time the genre has become crowded with titles, such that there is simply no need to tolerate these things. Other games (e.g. song of syx) have really nailed the large colony niche, so I'm not really sure what future this game has.

I like this style of game a lot, and I think this game has a lot of promise. However, it lacks a certain amount of polish that would be  needed for me to recommend. The micromanagement seems a little bit clumsy, especially compared to the likes of Rimworld - there are things I would just like more granular control of.

More importantly, there are some things that just seem inherently flawed. I quit after completing act 2 in the story mission, because it seemed stupidly frustrating that after relocating base from an apocalyptic scenario, all of my settlers become immediately depressed that they don't have their own bedroom. I don't have time to build everyone a bedroom, guys!! But every time I tried, one of my guys would become insane and hostile because of depression. Easy fix to just give a 'new base' buff to allow some time to build stuff. 

Little things like that are what pushes me to not recommend yet.

I have no idea what happened, was playing the game and all was going ok and then all of a sudden the difficulty shot through the roof. Constant raids, often back-to-back with no time to recover between ( like not even enough to heal my dudes ), the final doom of my colony was 3 raids and 1 animal attack all happening *at the same time*. No idea what broke the game enough to cause this, had played many hours with raids happening rarely but enough to pose some challenge, then BAM! The game wanted me dead. Oh well, I have other games to play.

No idea how this can be "1.0". Doesn't even work on larger resolutions properly (tons of stuff overflowing/overlapping/etc.). 

Dev also seems to be responding really badly to reviews (saw an instance where he basically just attacked the reviewer without addressing a single point, for example), so I'll probably just refund this later instead of waiting for it to get better (which would surprise me if it did, considering the current level of quality as a 1.0 product).

I tried it out, and it really does have potential. But as the game stands now, it is not worth the price it has. Too clunky AI, horrible combat. Tried to send pawns to deconstruct stuff in buildings around my base, they went halfway there before walking back to eat, then the same thing after eating. No thanks

It has a primitive, saddening UI. Looks like something released in 2012, not 2022. I tried to like it, but I dropped any interest in the middle of the tutorial. The final straw was the happy face balloon being drawn under the wall texture, and not above it as intended. For someone like me who has played thousands of hours of Rimworld, this game is nowhere pleasant to play.

[h1]Very early EA[/h1]

The dev is known for his X Mods, so i guess he will develope a decent game too.

Nontheless, i requested a refund because the game is very barebone with a clunky ui. No biggy since its pre-aplha but i had enough EA games and i prefer a more flashed out stage to join.

Another thing that drives me away is that the game is a night simulator. If you have no lights, the screen is very dark and that sux. I like nighttime, i'm comfortable with having a special mood or when certain traits are reduced eg perception/visibility. But i promised myself never to by a back screen simulator again and i will fulfill this one ;)

A 2nd big aspect i don't like is having traits like depression. I think these mental states are represented way too shallow in games. And here again, fight boredom and depression with entertainment buildings. I know it can change a lot but in the barebone state it is its rather a no go for me. Perhaps i just played too much of these games in the 1990's because we didn't have better gfx :P I mean u see whats going on on the screen and its decent for the current state but to get me hooked it has to deliver more in terms of society building and more meaningful mechanism for the traits your ppl have. I really tried to like this game.  It's a lot like Rimworld, and even adds a number of fun features that Rimworld lacks, but it's just not there.  I find myself struggling with the UI on small things, but it happens frequently enough that it's noticeable.  

My biggest issues are with the fights.  Your characters respond poorly during combat, and their AI is terrible.  It can be frustrating to see if you've selected a character, and you issue commands only to find that they weren't selected after all.  Getting characters to attack the right targets is painful, and enemies do a ton of damage, easily killing characters without much you can do about it.  Combat encounters can also be extremely imbalanced - I've had to reload several times in the early game when I was attacked by fully armed and armored assault groups that my people couldn't do anything to.  The game seems to encourage thinking that your people are completely expendable, and this doesn't fit my play style well.

Feature-wise, the game is great, but ultimately I just felt like I was constantly chasing a good time that was just out of reach.  The game is probably salvageable and there's a great game under it, but it's just not fun at the moment.

Its like an inferior more buggy version of Rimworld.

It does have an interesting setting, with more a post-nuclear zombie apocalypse feel to it, which is nice if you like that.
The major issues that makes this a not recommend is the unfinished, slow and confused AI, and the clunky UI.

-Workers continually get stuck anywhere and everywhere for no reason. My entire colony stopped working at one point because I was building a fence around my farm. That fence didn't block anyone from moving anywhere except inside the fence.
-The soldiers are difficult and slow to move around for combat, which will get you killed over and over again. Prepare to load a lot because of that.
-I can't stop my master chef from deciding she wants to put out a forest fire with her bare hands, causing her to now be indistinguishable from the infected, due to her rotted burned skin.
-Clicking space for pause is slow and only works depending on where your mouse is placed at the moment, so you find yourself clicking space over and over until its finally paused and you accidentally unpause it at the same time.
-One time my people were starving. All they had to do was go pick the already grown potatoes, ready for harvest. But being hungry had put them in a bad mood so they chose to starve to death instead.

The game shouldn't have been released until these issues were fixed, but I'm guessing the dev was tired of working on this, or just didn't know how to fix these issues. It should still be in early access.

The settings for a new game doesn't even seem to work right. I turned of dangerous weather for my first play and my crops still got destroyed by acid rain. Having no spare seeds meant that was game over.

Have to admit I laughed when I got attacked by a group of giant crabs early in the game, and one of them suddenly threw a fireball at my group, followed by them standing in the fire and getting more burned.
The game wants you to micromanage this stuff but doing something like moving 1 unit can be clunky, you may accidentally move the whole group.

Some good things:
- The soundtrack. Its eerie and fits the setting.
- Having multiple layers of the map was interesting.
- Seems to be a big world with lots of content.

Basically its almost a recommend. If the dev could just fix up the weird UI and improve pathfinding for workers.

After the collapse is a top down colony management game. People who have played DF / Rimworld will be familiar with the basic loop of the game. ATC adds mechanics of exploring the over world which gives you locations to loot and taking over giving you bonus resources etc. Beyond this there is unfortunately not much more innovation in this (now somewhat surprisingly) crowded genre, and plenty of things that it does not do as well as other titles - which is a shame as you can really tell the dev is highly passionate and trying to really make something great. 

Pros:
- Content: There is a decent amount of content there at the moment. Things to build, craft, research, grow and use. There are events, attacks to defend, and levels in your map to explore.
- Overmap: The overmap and related mechanics of looting and taking over are pretty neat and work well mechanically
- Z Layers: The game has multiple z layers which the genre has been moving away from, but unless the maps are special its only 3, and they arent really that well integrated into the game. 
- Performance: The game seems to perform well, even with large base numbers. 

Cons:
- Poor worker AI: Workers tend to pick the next job at random (within their current list of highest priority jobs) irrespective of the distance. This means they will craft 1 thing from a crafting table, then walk across the base to another crafting table and craft 1 thing. This seems to be true of everything (building, crafting, farming) and just destroys productivity. The dev seems to be putting in band aids (recent patch allows you to limit crafting table to one person) instead of addressing the core job issues, which means its likely a core design issue. 
- Poor worker Logistics: When building walls etc, the worker will take the stone for that wall and no other, build the wall then walk back and take the stone for the next wall etc. The dev seems to have fixed this for roofing but nothing else for some reason.
- Poor UI: The UI completely ignores the last 10 years of UI lessons learned in colony sims and forges its own path in terms of almost everything with unfortunately predictably poor results. Setting jobs for colonists is needlessly confusing. Basic things like editing crafting numbers cannot be done (instead the job must be deleted and recreated). Key submenus hidden behind nonsensical menus (i.e. the job manager is behind the factions menu). Zones cannot be edited, only deleted and recreated (which for farming destroys the existing crop)
- Combat: Combat is very fast, which means there is little time for strategy or manoeuvre, its just spray and pray. 

Overall ATC started as an interesting alternative take on the colony management genre, but poor AI and UI have prevented it from really taking off. At the start these downsides would have been tolerated but in the mean time the genre has become crowded with titles, such that there is simply no need to tolerate these things. Other games (e.g. song of syx) have really nailed the large colony niche, so I'm not really sure what future this game has.

I like this style of game a lot, and I think this game has a lot of promise. However, it lacks a certain amount of polish that would be  needed for me to recommend. The micromanagement seems a little bit clumsy, especially compared to the likes of Rimworld - there are things I would just like more granular control of.

More importantly, there are some things that just seem inherently flawed. I quit after completing act 2 in the story mission, because it seemed stupidly frustrating that after relocating base from an apocalyptic scenario, all of my settlers become immediately depressed that they don't have their own bedroom. I don't have time to build everyone a bedroom, guys!! But every time I tried, one of my guys would become insane and hostile because of depression. Easy fix to just give a 'new base' buff to allow some time to build stuff. 

Little things like that are what pushes me to not recommend yet.

I have no idea what happened, was playing the game and all was going ok and then all of a sudden the difficulty shot through the roof. Constant raids, often back-to-back with no time to recover between ( like not even enough to heal my dudes ), the final doom of my colony was 3 raids and 1 animal attack all happening *at the same time*. No idea what broke the game enough to cause this, had played many hours with raids happening rarely but enough to pose some challenge, then BAM! The game wanted me dead. Oh well, I have other games to play.

No idea how this can be "1.0". Doesn't even work on larger resolutions properly (tons of stuff overflowing/overlapping/etc.). 

Dev also seems to be responding really badly to reviews (saw an instance where he basically just attacked the reviewer without addressing a single point, for example), so I'll probably just refund this later instead of waiting for it to get better (which would surprise me if it did, considering the current level of quality as a 1.0 product).

Positives:
1. The combat is incredibly fun.
2. I dig the art style.

Negatives:
1. The shop isn't up in EA? Seems like a weird thing to leave out.
2. The mod system isn't available in EA either? Just missing a huge chunk of the customization.
3. The game constantly hard freezes, getting tired of restarting.
4. Still progressing through the story, but really hoping there's some kind of end game loop.
5. The price tag is kinda ridiculous considering what's available... really should have a lot more for the price.

DO NOT  BUY THIS GAME, IT IS A SCAM. For $35, you will get scammed. The progression and customization mechanics are all currently locked under "not available in early access". A proper price to charge for this game currently would be $0. I played numerous Steam Next Fest demos last week that had more content than this  product-- a product asking you to pay more than you have to for Cyber Punk Phantom Liberty. 

As for what IS here: 
1) Ballistics on guns are awful. Feels like you're firing spitballs. 
2) Enemy variety is abysmal. Same mechanics required to defeat all enemies 
3) Level design is loathsome. Huge, totally vacant rooms with a couple of indestructible barrels in the corner. The rooms contain no objects which provide any insight into what exactly is meant to be happening within them. Is this a boiler room? A living quarters? An office? They might as well all be bathrooms because this game belongs in the toilet. 

This extends to further demonstrate the staggeringly contrived and cliche nature of this game. The world? I guess general spaceship. The enemies? Generally bug zombie. Why we're here? Because you're a big robot guy with a gun on a spaceship. 

Let me reiterate, the most important thing for any isometric shooter to do in order to even stand it’s ground as a worthy product is to make the guns feel good to shoot. That is it. If your starting weapon feels good to shoot, you can get hooked. Once I left clicked upon entering the first level and felt the little bubble blowing puff that popped out of my massive, techno looking assault rifle, I knew that I was dealing with an anateurish game at best and an unfinished attempt at quick cash at worst. If you’re looking for how dynamic, engaging, and complex gunplay can truly elevate a top-down shooter experience, play Synthetik. Recoil, deviation, firerate, jamming, heat production, handling, ammo type, all that stuff that makes guns fun to shoot is integral to that title. Here in Dreadhunter you’re farting out a line of skittles at the nondescript bug zombies for no other reason than you were either stupid enough or bored enough to waste your time on a game that the devs couldn’t be bothered to spend their time making. 


This game looks and plays like something that an AI made in 20 minutes. The price its currently at is borderline theft. Do not support these devs. Their ability to get away with this con-job hinges on you not refunding the game if you buy it. They don’t deserve you to give them that satisfaction and they need to either be boxed out of this industry or actually invest their time and creative energy into this lest they get away with making money off garbage.
When I started picking up items only to look at them in the menus to see “not available in EA” I wondered why even put them as drops…and why did I pay this much for so little?

Also its straight up missing a ton of content such as mods and crafting and market.

for the price it simply isnt worth

Sadly the price is too high for the quantity of content at the moment.
Wait for a sale or at least wait for some updates.
Every level feels the same.

So far I'm not sure if I should recommend or not so, decide for yourself.

DO NOT  BUY THIS GAME, IT IS A SCAM. For $35, you will get scammed. The progression and customization mechanics are all currently locked under "not available in early access". A proper price to charge for this game currently would be $0. I played numerous Steam Next Fest demos last week that had more content than this  product-- a product asking you to pay more than you have to for Cyber Punk Phantom Liberty. 

As for what IS here: 
1) Ballistics on guns are awful. Feels like you're firing spitballs. 
2) Enemy variety is abysmal. Same mechanics required to defeat all enemies 
3) Level design is loathsome. Huge, totally vacant rooms with a couple of indestructible barrels in the corner. The rooms contain no objects which provide any insight into what exactly is meant to be happening within them. Is this a boiler room? A living quarters? An office? They might as well all be bathrooms because this game belongs in the toilet. 

This extends to further demonstrate the staggeringly contrived and cliche nature of this game. The world? I guess general spaceship. The enemies? Generally bug zombie. Why we're here? Because you're a big robot guy with a gun on a spaceship. 

Let me reiterate, the most important thing for any isometric shooter to do in order to even stand it’s ground as a worthy product is to make the guns feel good to shoot. That is it. If your starting weapon feels good to shoot, you can get hooked. Once I left clicked upon entering the first level and felt the little bubble blowing puff that popped out of my massive, techno looking assault rifle, I knew that I was dealing with an anateurish game at best and an unfinished attempt at quick cash at worst. If you’re looking for how dynamic, engaging, and complex gunplay can truly elevate a top-down shooter experience, play Synthetik. Recoil, deviation, firerate, jamming, heat production, handling, ammo type, all that stuff that makes guns fun to shoot is integral to that title. Here in Dreadhunter you’re farting out a line of skittles at the nondescript bug zombies for no other reason than you were either stupid enough or bored enough to waste your time on a game that the devs couldn’t be bothered to spend their time making.

[b][i]Pocket Rogues[/i] isn't particularly bad, and the price-point is surely fine - but it's just too standard in it's design.[/b] What we have here is a permanent upgrade system like in titles such as [i]Rogue Legacy[/i] or [i]Immortal Redneck[/i], mixed with some vanilla aRPG dungeon crawling. And it's all just a bit too slow for my tastes. It felt like the runs were watered down by being far too easy until you hit a wall. I would prefer a more potent experience where I die sooner and earn more gold in the meantime. Tighter loops; shorter runs. Now I'm sure this is a labor-of-love project with modest ambition - and I appreciate and applaud that. I just don't find it compelling and therefore can't recommend my friends run out and put down their money.

[b]While we've been over this before - I have to again reiterate that this particular type of permanent upgrade system is tricky in that it doesn't so much reward skill, as it does grind.[/b] You grind to get permanent upgrades and buffs, in turn to grind to get further in the dungeon. It can work, but in general this is my least favorite way to manage upgrades. The upgrades are all standard, too. We have stereotypical upgrades like damage buffs, and classic playable classes. This is an easy and free opportunity to make this title stand out from the sea of dungeon crawlers out there.

[b]There are rough edges.[/b] Sure, it's an Early Access campaign. I get that. Controller support is pretty wonky right now. Many of the enemy sprites are muddy and really hard to tell what they are. The enemy AI is quite bad (they get stuck on corners and walls). Building your fortress sounds great, but it really just boils down to a tech/skill tree. Level design is nothing special. Again, I'm not trying to hate on a pet project, but all these things add up to something I can live without. 

[b]Pros:[/b]
+I can't argue with the price
+I do like the idea of a bite-size aRPG rogue-ish game

[b]Cons:[/b]
-There is nothing fresh here
-Overly standard dungeon crawler
-The enemy artwork is muddy
-Controller support needs a good bit of work
-The dungeon crawling is a bit too slow, and runs too long for my tastes

[b]It isn't about the money with this one, it's about your time.[/b] I do wish the developer the best with this and any future projects - but this gets a hard pass from me.

Game looks fun at first, have steady progression, untill you hit a wall after you unlock all fungeons (and you do that kinda fast). then...you just repeat same dungeons. And yah, i know, thats what all these kind of games are about. But deeper you go, the "same" it is, upgrades are simple (+ stats), artifacts are uninteresting and only few (and again, auto gold pickup, more gold, more exp), items also same, higher quality gives you more buffs, but they are boring. After playing it ~20h (both pc and mobile), i got frustrated, tryed different classes, but its still lacks depth. And then i did noughty part - cheated, made myself a lot of gold, gems, skill points. After some calculation i understood that to max all things you need insane amount of gold and skill points, and each upgrade gives you abysmall progression. Dungeons get repetitive fast, mobs are always same in endless dungeons just get bigger numbers. Good potential, but mid - end game is just...unfinished. Would not recomend at current state

I have to agree with the big review that's currently marked as the most helpful.  It's not that there's anything bad about this game, it's just not particularly interesting.  Despite the absurdly low price, i wouldn't really recommend it.  

For me, the game became a bit tedious almost immediately.  If there's one big complaint i have, it's just that the game is too slow.  All of my initial upgrades were into Agility, with the hope that having my character move faster would be a relief.  But the points required to upgrade go up by level, and the stat itself only increments by 0.5.  This means that you spend something like 30 upgrade points to get an increase of 3 or 4% in speed.  And i don't believe i got more than 10 or 12 points to spend per run.  I'm sure it's possible to get more, but i just lost patience with the pace and started leaping into battle with every enemy in the room to relieve the monotony.

I totally understand the generally positive reviews here.  The game is cheap, and it gives you lots of upgrades to spend points and money on.  But for me, this is way to slow and grindy.
Forever in EA, save resets.
Creating an account just straight up doesnt work at all no matter what i try to do so there goes any thoughts of multiplayer, Tried everything under the sun to get it working but to no avail.

As it stands right now, Pocket Rogues is too buggy to recommend. 

Menus taking 3 key-presses to open, hits not registering, and to top it off; My game soft-locked after I beat the first boss and I couldn't go down into the next room, thus nullifying any progress I'd made so far.

The game has some good ideas, I just hope the dev sorts out these game-breaking bugs.

Overrated,

difficulty level does not rise properly. on your first level of a run, almost naked without a potion u find a hardened level 21 bada** killer and thats not just once or twice is almost 1 out of 3 tries. 

As a dungeon crawler u must
      - Rouge like: level dificcult increase must be proportional
      - Action RPG: u dont lose everything when die

I dont matter grinding dungeons once and again as Diablo I requested but once I hit the roof, just rerun without lossing everything. 
I dont matter either rerun once and again the same dungeon with diferent loot options.

More: RoI is bad as hell. Invest 12K to earn 300 per hour needs 40h to recover the investment. Invest 40K to earn 400 per hour needs 100h to recover the investment. Go f**k your self.

It worked the first time I installed it but now when I launch it I get a black screen with the game's cursor and nothing else.  I've left it in this state for several minutes with no change.  My GPU is pegged at 100% due to Pocket Rogues.  Uninstalled and reinstalled with the same result.  If this is the standard behavior on a PC I can't recommend it to others.

(refunded)

The game literally does not allow you to play with others even though stated that it's apart of the game. We keep getting "login failed" and "invalid steam ticket" errors when attempting to create an account.
Sprinting costs mana. Necromancer is laughably bad. Bosses are so spastic, you can't tell if they're just moving, or kicking your ass.
After years of playing I've figured out (the hard way) that my saves were no cloud. I've lost everything. I cannot recommend.

I have to agree with the big review that's currently marked as the most helpful.  It's not that there's anything bad about this game, it's just not particularly interesting.  Despite the absurdly low price, i wouldn't really recommend it.  

For me, the game became a bit tedious almost immediately.  If there's one big complaint i have, it's just that the game is too slow.  All of my initial upgrades were into Agility, with the hope that having my character move faster would be a relief.  But the points required to upgrade go up by level, and the stat itself only increments by 0.5.  This means that you spend something like 30 upgrade points to get an increase of 3 or 4% in speed.  And i don't believe i got more than 10 or 12 points to spend per run.  I'm sure it's possible to get more, but i just lost patience with the pace and started leaping into battle with every enemy in the room to relieve the monotony.

I totally understand the generally positive reviews here.  The game is cheap, and it gives you lots of upgrades to spend points and money on.  But for me, this is way to slow and grindy.
Forever in EA, save resets.
Creating an account just straight up doesnt work at all no matter what i try to do so there goes any thoughts of multiplayer, Tried everything under the sun to get it working but to no avail.

As it stands right now, Pocket Rogues is too buggy to recommend. 

Menus taking 3 key-presses to open, hits not registering, and to top it off; My game soft-locked after I beat the first boss and I couldn't go down into the next room, thus nullifying any progress I'd made so far.

The game has some good ideas, I just hope the dev sorts out these game-breaking bugs.

[b][i]Pocket Rogues[/i] isn't particularly bad, and the price-point is surely fine - but it's just too standard in it's design.[/b] What we have here is a permanent upgrade system like in titles such as [i]Rogue Legacy[/i] or [i]Immortal Redneck[/i], mixed with some vanilla aRPG dungeon crawling. And it's all just a bit too slow for my tastes. It felt like the runs were watered down by being far too easy until you hit a wall. I would prefer a more potent experience where I die sooner and earn more gold in the meantime. Tighter loops; shorter runs. Now I'm sure this is a labor-of-love project with modest ambition - and I appreciate and applaud that. I just don't find it compelling and therefore can't recommend my friends run out and put down their money.

[b]While we've been over this before - I have to again reiterate that this particular type of permanent upgrade system is tricky in that it doesn't so much reward skill, as it does grind.[/b] You grind to get permanent upgrades and buffs, in turn to grind to get further in the dungeon. It can work, but in general this is my least favorite way to manage upgrades. The upgrades are all standard, too. We have stereotypical upgrades like damage buffs, and classic playable classes. This is an easy and free opportunity to make this title stand out from the sea of dungeon crawlers out there.

[b]There are rough edges.[/b] Sure, it's an Early Access campaign. I get that. Controller support is pretty wonky right now. Many of the enemy sprites are muddy and really hard to tell what they are. The enemy AI is quite bad (they get stuck on corners and walls). Building your fortress sounds great, but it really just boils down to a tech/skill tree. Level design is nothing special. Again, I'm not trying to hate on a pet project, but all these things add up to something I can live without. 

[b]Pros:[/b]
+I can't argue with the price
+I do like the idea of a bite-size aRPG rogue-ish game

[b]Cons:[/b]
-There is nothing fresh here
-Overly standard dungeon crawler
-The enemy artwork is muddy
-Controller support needs a good bit of work
-The dungeon crawling is a bit too slow, and runs too long for my tastes

[b]It isn't about the money with this one, it's about your time.[/b] I do wish the developer the best with this and any future projects - but this gets a hard pass from me.

It took me 14 hours to get through the whole story but it felt longer since I felt like nothing new was really happening until the very end. I felt like this game would have played with the heart strings a little but in the end there wasn't a scene that left an impact on me.

Now the music was good but it doesn't have the effect like the music from other visual novel's where I'd remember a specific moment in the game whenever the song comes on. I do love the character designs as well as their personality, lots of fun and cute moments which kept me reading. The ability to have JP and ENG text at the same time was a nice surprise. 

Overall I just wouldn't recommend it at the current price, definitely would on a sale though.

mmmm I want to say that I enjoyed the game but generally speaking I felt that almost every reveal/twist/development I've seen done better elsewhere so it kind of lacked any sort of identity to me. Ginka is cute sure but that can't hold an entire game. I liked ATRI a lot and still feel it is pretty novel with what it did but with GINKA I came away feeling just sort of mixed. The art is certainly beautiful though, thanks Yusano. 
I feel this is a game that I would have enjoy more if I read it 10+ years ago when I was still just getting into VNs.

mmmm I want to say that I enjoyed the game but generally speaking I felt that almost every reveal/twist/development I've seen done better elsewhere so it kind of lacked any sort of identity to me. Ginka is cute sure but that can't hold an entire game. I liked ATRI a lot and still feel it is pretty novel with what it did but with GINKA I came away feeling just sort of mixed. The art is certainly beautiful though, thanks Yusano. 
I feel this is a game that I would have enjoy more if I read it 10+ years ago when I was still just getting into VNs.

It took me 14 hours to get through the whole story but it felt longer since I felt like nothing new was really happening until the very end. I felt like this game would have played with the heart strings a little but in the end there wasn't a scene that left an impact on me.

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text: Just like the first game, this one is chock full of BDSM. Nearly every scene involves ropes or beatings, and it's mandatory. Dear devs, you did a solid job (for a hentai game), but you really should list fetishes in the description or the about section.

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text:
This series was great, but the devs have gone full-on into A.I. images with minor touch ups instead of hand-drawn stuff and it shows (Seriously, you can tell a glance. It's genuinely horrible even by A.I. standards). 

While it's limited in this game, only being in the expansion, it is quite prevalent in the upcoming squeal as well as their other new titles. It's a damn shame too, this used to be my favorite Hentai series...

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text:
TLDR : just play Fap mode and enjoy a cutsceene

Don't waste your time playing this game, the level design are broke, enemy isn't smart but they are cheating with tons of their spawn point.

Play fap mode and enjoy, your time are more precious than grinding in H game

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text:
The game play is quite repetitive. The H scenes are mediocre and you can't skip the audio. And compared to the amount of battles, the H scenes are few. But most importantly, the plots of H scenes don't make any sense.... The script writer just arbitrarily find some reasons for them to have sex. 
I know that it's cheap and there are quite a lot of gaming content for such price. But I simply think it's a waste of time. After all, it is not so good as either a H game or a strategy game.

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text: Its hard to say not to get this but there is so much that makes it easy to say it. maybe after a year or so of many many things being tested and fixed it could be a yes. the items are meh, the gems are about useless, and the units are laughable. there are 9 different units in the game(at least what they make u think). there a 3 at first, swordsmen, gunmen,carvery. then u unlock 3 more that is just like them but different names that cost more and very little stat difference. now the enemy get the big old war elephants that they use all the time. they will DESTROY every unit u send out. i have seen them one shot many of my units. your last unit.......lol is a healer..haha. yep a squish healer that rarely heals. no war bears or war dogs. no berserk, no awesome units. just plain old jane units. But there is one awesome unit......the War elephants, do u get it..lol heck no. o and the merge gems and combine gem is a joke. i mean hard joke. i combined two rare gems and got a really crapy common. not like hey this common isnt to bad, i mean like why did they put this gem in the game common. all in all it has so much potential but it shouldnt have even been brought out yet. there is no flair, no imagination. but hey there is War Elephants! just not for u!

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text: Boring, buggy, repetitive, easy as hell even on hard diff (basically you win fight in one minute)

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text:
Good game even without the hentei content.
Unfortunately, there is no information that there is MISSING CONTENT FOR A PRODUCT I PAYED FOR.

The game is good, really good.
It is similar to plants vs zombies. Diffrent units on diffrent lanes.
Counter possibilities, upgrades for units, skills for the generals make this a very fun and strategic experience.
It is certaily worth the price.
 
But if I do not get the full product, I will not recommend it.

review_text: Its hard to say not to get this but there is so much that makes it easy to say it. maybe after a year or so of many many things being tested and fixed it could be a yes. the items are meh, the gems are about useless, and the units are laughable. there are 9 different units in the game(at least what they make u think). there a 3 at first, swordsmen, gunmen,carvery. then u unlock 3 more that is just like them but different names that cost more and very little stat difference. now the enemy get the big old war elephants that they use all the time. they will DESTROY every unit u send out. i have seen them one shot many of my units. your last unit.......lol is a healer..haha. yep a squish healer that rarely heals. no war bears or war dogs. no berserk, no awesome units. just plain old jane units. But there is one awesome unit......the War elephants, do u get it..lol heck no. o and the merge gems and combine gem is a joke. i mean hard joke. i combined two rare gems and got a really crapy common. not like hey this common isnt to bad, i mean like why did they put this gem in the game common. all in all it has so much potential but it shouldnt have even been brought out yet. there is no flair, no imagination. but hey there is War Elephants! just not for u!

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text: Boring, buggy, repetitive, easy as hell even on hard diff (basically you win fight in one minute)

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text:
Good game even without the hentei content.
Unfortunately, there is no information that there is MISSING CONTENT FOR A PRODUCT I PAYED FOR.

The game is good, really good.
It is similar to plants vs zombies. Diffrent units on diffrent lanes.
Counter possibilities, upgrades for units, skills for the generals make this a very fun and strategic experience.
It is certaily worth the price.
 
But if I do not get the full product, I will not recommend it.

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text:
This series was great, but the devs have gone full-on into A.I. images with minor touch ups instead of hand-drawn stuff and it shows (Seriously, you can tell a glance. It's genuinely horrible even by A.I. standards). 

While it's limited in this game, only being in the expansion, it is quite prevalent in the upcoming squeal as well as their other new titles. It's a damn shame too, this used to be my favorite Hentai series...

review_text:
TLDR : just play Fap mode and enjoy a cutsceene

Play fap mode and enjoy, your time are more precious than grinding in H game

game_name: Love n War: Hero by Chance II
review_text:
The game play is quite repetitive. The H scenes are mediocre and you can't skip the audio. And compared to the amount of battles, the H scenes are few. But most importantly, the plots of H scenes don't make any sense.... The script writer just arbitrarily find some reasons for them to have sex. 
I know that it's cheap and there are quite a lot of gaming content for such price. But I simply think it's a waste of time. After all, it is not so good as either a H game or a strategy game.

Forsake is a Phasmophobia type clone where the player is an Urban Explorer hunting ghosts. Unfortunately, the scares is only a Grim Reaper type enemy, whom you can't hear, floating toward you in a dull and uneventful way, making the scares feel like a bore. 

The controls are annoying as the default buttons to lean are 'C' to lean right, and 'B' to lean left. The graphics seems like the game is in Alpha stage instead of full release, and the gameplay is completely forgettable as the player would need to collect 3 relics in order to complete the map while being chased by a "ghost". 

Simply put, the controls are not good and the gameplay is lack luster to say the least. Its an immediate refund, even if it was on sale. DO NOT BUY!

I know that is early access but i can't play with 20 fps with nvidia gtx 1650. Developers pls fix bugs much soon as possible. For now who wants to play remember that after 2 hours of playing u woudn't return ur money.

The game has potential but after crashing multiple times and then losing the progress that I've made I cannot recommend the game in its current state.

The game is pretty boring at its current state. Since it's easy to keep track of the where monster is at all times, it's never really scary or interesting, you just keep looking for keys and codes, which gets old quick. 

Barely any effort put on models and animations, and environments are empty. Can't see the light coming of from other players flashlights.

[quote]
[h1]Forsake: Urban horror | By Wholf[/h1]
[list]
[*] Forsake is a 1 to 4-player co-op online psychological horror game where each player is a member of an urban explorer group. Enter places that are off-limits and full of paranormal activity to discover their darkest secrets, while risking your life. I and a friend bought it for 4 euros and the day after it went to 2,79 euros. Luckily we didn't play for over 2 hours due to the refund policy. But, let me explain a bit more about my experience and why I refunded it.

[*] So, I and a friend were bored and were looking for horror-type games that can be played together and we found Forsake: Urban horror which looked pretty interesting. Because it was quite cheap we bought and played together. Unfortunately, me and my friend had problems with the game. Changing options and graphics crashed our games multiple times. Since our PC's are quite high-end we wanted to play on everything high but this wasn't possible so we played with high resolution which still was fine-ish. 

[*] After around 20 minutes of trying to play the game we finally got to the point where we could play and my instant feeling was, "wtf am I playing". Why is this you say? Well, it was overall pretty damn cheap. The graphics and quality were very basic and bland, felt like a huge copy/pasted asset from the marketplace and I couldn't even see my friend his flashlight which normally should work so it was completely dark if we didn't turn on our flashlights. We started to play on Easy mode and yes. This was easy peasy indeed. You have a map where you can find everything basically. Keys, items to sell for money and, to buy other equipment the same as in Phasmophibia, even though you have grenades here and other weird items, dafuq?

[*] Completed the first map, second map, and third map in less than 1.3 ish hours as it was just way to easy. But of-course, we played in easy mode. Everything was just plain simple. The same as with the movement and everything in your surroundings like the trees, walls, objects and you name it. It unmotivated us. Now, let's talk about the part of the zombies/ghosts/monsters whatever you wanna call it. You will not get scared at all. It starts to get very simple and expected when the sound effects instantly play. You can jump on tables, chairs, furniture, and whatever you can find and the 'monster' isn't even killing you. It just goes straight passed you or stays there till you move away. 

[*] Overall, and I probably said it before. It's bland, kinda boring, copy/pasted asset-flip, no jumpscares, simple puzzles, a map, equipment which is nice but weird as well, there's a skill tree which is okay, and some basic characters you can play. It's nothing new in these genres/types of games. This was the reason we refunded it. I'd say this game is fun for 20 minutes and from there you'll understand everything that's going on.
[/list]
[/quote]

If trying to join a friends' room, just prompts with "Failed to find room". Still playable solo, but is very much not fun nor the intended way to play when buying.

Forsake is a Phasmophobia type clone where the player is an Urban Explorer hunting ghosts. Unfortunately, the scares is only a Grim Reaper type enemy, whom you can't hear, floating toward you in a dull and uneventful way, making the scares feel like a bore. 

The controls are annoying as the default buttons to lean are 'C' to lean right, and 'B' to lean left. The graphics seems like the game is in Alpha stage instead of full release, and the gameplay is completely forgettable as the player would need to collect 3 relics in order to complete the map while being chased by a "ghost". 

Simply put, the controls are not good and the gameplay is lack luster to say the least. Its an immediate refund, even if it was on sale. DO NOT BUY!

I bought the game and i steel want to pay money back because of this game still such a bad with bugs and non-optimised unity engine. I know that is early access but i can't play with 20 fps with nvidia gtx 1650. Developers pls fix bugs much soon as possible. For now who wants to play remember that after 2 hours of playing u woudn't return ur money.

The game has potential but after crashing multiple times and then losing the progress that I've made I cannot recommend the game in its current state.

Can shoot teammates with a gun though so that's cool though.

The game is pretty boring at its current state. Since it's easy to keep track of the where monster is at all times, it's never really scary or interesting, you just keep looking for keys and codes, which gets old quick. 

Barely any effort put on models and animations, and environments are empty. Can't see the light coming of from other players flashlights.

[quote]
[h1]Forsake: Urban horror | By Wholf[/h1]
[list]
[*] Forsake is a 1 to 4-player co-op online psychological horror game where each player is a member of an urban explorer group. Enter places that are off-limits and full of paranormal activity to discover their darkest secrets, while risking your life. I and a friend bought it for 4 euros and the day after it went to 2,79 euros. Luckily we didn't play for over 2 hours due to the refund policy. But, let me explain a bit more about my experience and why I refunded it.

[*] So, I and a friend were bored and were looking for horror-type games that can be played together and we found Forsake: Urban horror which looked pretty interesting. Because it was quite cheap we bought and played together. Unfortunately, me and my friend had problems with the game. Changing options and graphics crashed our games multiple times. Since our PC's are quite high-end we wanted to play on everything high but this wasn't possible so we played with high resolution which still was fine-ish. 

[*] After around 20 minutes of trying to play the game we finally got to the point where we could play and my instant feeling was, "wtf am I playing". Why is this you say? Well, it was overall pretty damn cheap. The graphics and quality were very basic and bland, felt like a huge copy/pasted asset from the marketplace and I couldn't even see my friend his flashlight which normally should work so it was completely dark if we didn't turn on our flashlights. We started to play on Easy mode and yes. This was easy peasy indeed. You have a map where you can find everything basically. Keys, items to sell for money and, to buy other equipment the same as in Phasmophibia, even though you have grenades here and other weird items, dafuq?

[*] Completed the first map, second map, and third map in less than 1.3 ish hours as it was just way to easy. But of-course, we played in easy mode. Everything was just plain simple. The same as with the movement and everything in your surroundings like the trees, walls, objects and you name it. It unmotivated us. Now, let's talk about the part of the zombies/ghosts/monsters whatever you wanna call it. You will not get scared at all. It starts to get very simple and expected when the sound effects instantly play. You can jump on tables, chairs, furniture, and whatever you can find and the 'monster' isn't even killing you. It just goes straight passed you or stays there till you move away. 

[*] Overall, and I probably said it before. It's bland, kinda boring, copy/pasted asset-flip, no jumpscares, simple puzzles, a map, equipment which is nice but weird as well, there's a skill tree which is okay, and some basic characters you can play. It's nothing new in these genres/types of games. This was the reason we refunded it. I'd say this game is fun for 20 minutes and from there you'll understand everything that's going on.
[/list]
[/quote]

I LOVE POC "Classic"!
I was totally happy to find the game on Steam. 
Sure its technically polished and totally worth to pay the same price from 25 years before again ...I thought.

No, it is NOT. 
You can't proper use the menu with the mouse (kb is okay), can't turn off the sound afaik and there is not even a full-screen switch. Even the original was full-screen. 

I still love the game. But for this price you can really await much more polish. 
Sorry dudes, but maybe for half of the price.

Somewhere in the back was a good idea, but the execution failed. I can't complete all the tasks (i only found 3 tables to clean first night), no flashlight and you can't see much, the graphics are bad. I couldn't feel the atmosphere, i think game give more fun where watching on youtube.

I got all of the tasks done except for the trash, but it would not let me get the soda can in the custodial room. I’m not sure if this is just a bug or what, but it did not allow me to complete the game.

Where do I start?  * Doesn't appear as a "Perfect Game" when all Steam Achievements are obtained. *  The last 4 or 5 times I tried to play the game, I was pinned at the beginning spawn point. After getting stuck in the clogged toilet stall & having to restart on Shift 3. Whether continuing or starting a new game, I came back to being pinned at spawn. 
 The environment is really well done, artistically & layout wise but what I don't understand is whyTF it is so damn empty. There are so many opportunities for jump scares, yet there are very few. The game is super quiet, there's no background music or random spoopy sounds, only sounds with actions & events. The spooky events that do happen are done well but they are few & far between. Lots of lost opportunities here.
 There was one classroom where if I was too close to the window I ended up outside the map of the school & was never able to get back in except if I restarted. It was cool hanging out with Jeff though. Check out the video here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Cumo9Z0guBM&t=156s
I got the game for less than $5 & I am not even sure I would recommend it at that point.  Probably skip this one unless you wanna see how bad it actually is.
Go find the pixel of trash somewhere in an extremely dark game. Then do it 17 more times. Then do it again on Night 2.  That's as far as I got. I don't know how many nights there are.

Its just meh city

3/10 for good atmosphere

I got all of the tasks done except for the trash, but it would not let me get the soda can in the custodial room. I’m not sure if this is just a bug or what, but it did not allow me to complete the game.

Where do I start?  * Doesn't appear as a "Perfect Game" when all Steam Achievements are obtained. *  The last 4 or 5 times I tried to play the game, I was pinned at the beginning spawn point. After getting stuck in the clogged toilet stall & having to restart on Shift 3. Whether continuing or starting a new game, I came back to being pinned at spawn. 
 The environment is really well done, artistically & layout wise but what I don't understand is whyTF it is so damn empty. There are so many opportunities for jump scares, yet there are very few. The game is super quiet, there's no background music or random spoopy sounds, only sounds with actions & events. The spooky events that do happen are done well but they are few & far between. Lots of lost opportunities here.
 There was one classroom where if I was too close to the window I ended up outside the map of the school & was never able to get back in except if I restarted. It was cool hanging out with Jeff though. Check out the video here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Cumo9Z0guBM&t=156s
I got the game for less than $5 & I am not even sure I would recommend it at that point.  Probably skip this one unless you wanna see how bad it actually is.
Go find the pixel of trash somewhere in an extremely dark game. Then do it 17 more times. Then do it again on Night 2.  That's as far as I got. I don't know how many nights there are.

Its just meh city
